title: BEM-915 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM915_WORKER_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-915 WORKER TELEGRAM OPERATOR INTERFACE REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- Operator remark is valid. The current Telegram channel behaves like a passive hourly notifier and does not yet provide a working operator interface.
- The Telegram layer must be treated as a first-class external interface of the managing contour.
- The bot should not only send 'no changes' reports. It must support task intake, acknowledgements, status tracking, gate approvals/rejections, and role-based reports.
- The Telegram interface must map every operator message to a structured envelope with trace_id, task_id, source, target role/object, priority, policy decision and proof path.
- Incoming Telegram tasks should route through the same managed channel, policy gate and dispatch queue already defined in BEM-914.
- Reports should have levels: Curator operational report, Director planning/rules report, General Director strategic/gate report.
- Bot must provide correlation IDs and must confirm delivery or failure.
Autonomous scope:
- Protocol design, command grammar, envelope schema, routing table, error model, report templates, deterministic parser stub, E2E selftest plan.
Operator gate:
- Real Telegram token/secrets, production webhook changes, issue #31 write actions, schedule triggers, and any always-on daemon model.
Recommendation:
- Approve updated protocol with notes.
- Status: TELEGRAM_PROTOCOL_APPROVED_WITH_OPERATOR_GATES.
No issue comments.
